overall_summary: ElizaOS is nearing the release of Version 3 and Eliza Cloud while undergoing a major ecosystem-wide dependency modernization targeting Node.js 24 and TypeScript 6. Community sentiment is currently split between technical progress and significant concern regarding token price performance.
key_facts: Contributor lalalune initiated an ecosystem-wide dependency modernization targeting Node.js 24, TypeScript 6, and Rimraf 6 across all core repositories.
ElizaOS Version 3 and Eliza Cloud are nearing final release, with the latter already accepting business clients for direct app integration.
The Supabase/Postgres Docker dependency was updated to version 17.6.1.112 to improve system stability.
A framework update involving $degenai is currently in progress by the core development team.
Technical implementation of Hierarchical Task Networks (HTN) has been added to ElizaOS v2 for agent goal decomposition.
Moderators formed the 'eliza army' steering group to improve community coordination and project involvement.
The team established a token utility strategy of using revenue from any payment method to perform token buybacks rather than enforcing token-specific friction.
GitHub PAT persistence was added for coding sub-agents to improve development automation workflows.
open_questions: How do the specific implementation details of the HTN upgrade in ElizaOS v2 compare to full HTN planning?
Is anyone having issues with eliza rn?

new_issues_prs.title: V2.0.0 release
new_issues_prs.author: odilitime
new_issues_prs.significance: Major modernization of repo automation, CI consolidation, and multi-language support for v2.0.0.

new_issues_prs.title: feat: pipeline hooks
new_issues_prs.author: Not specified
new_issues_prs.significance: Introduces hooks for prompt optimization and plugin enhancements.

new_issues_prs.title: fix(core): prevent duplicate LLM calls when message contains URL
new_issues_prs.author: nicolasdma
new_issues_prs.significance: Critical cost-saving fix that prevents redundant streaming and token usage.

overall_focus.claim: Development focus centered on monetization infrastructure for Eliza Cloud, agent connectivity improvements, and major dependency modernization.

discord_updates.summary: Discussions were dominated by token price performance and requests for VC partnerships, alongside educational sessions on the 1966 ELIZA chatbot and the ELIZA effect.
Technical deep dive into Hierarchical Task Networks (HTN) and their implementation path from HTN-lite to full LLM-based planning in ElizaOS v2.

user_feedback.feedback_summary: Community members expressed financial frustration due to the token market cap dropping below $5 million.
Users reported the Milady Play Store link in the GitHub repository is non-functional.

strategic_insights.insight: By choosing to accept any payment method and performing buybacks rather than requiring the native token for utility, the team is prioritizing user onboarding and revenue generation over forced token velocity.
implications_or_questions: Will the community accept this indirect utility model in the face of current price declines?

market_analysis.observation: Token market cap reported under $5 million with community calls for outreach to ai16z and Pantera Capital.
market_analysis.relevance: Directly impacts community morale and pressure for 'market-making' interventions.
